chapter: Chapter 1: The Ever-Evolving World of Science

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the scientific method?

options.A: Collecting evidence and proving a belief

questions.explanation: The scientific method involves observation, forming a hypothesis, experimentation, and conclusion.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a scientific law?

options.A: Law of Gravity

questions.explanation: A scientific law describes a pattern observed in nature, like the Law of Gravity.

questions.question: Why are scientific theories sometimes modified?

options.A: Because scientists make mistakes
options.B: Because new evidence or experiments may provide better explanations

questions.explanation: Scientific theories evolve as new evidence or better explanations emerge.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of observational science?

options.A: Studying the stars and planets in astronomy
options.C: Developing a medicine in a lab

questions.explanation: Observational science relies on observing natural phenomena, like in astronomy.

questions.question: Which is a correct distinction between science and technology?

options.A: Science solves practical problems, technology creates knowledge
options.B: Science seeks knowledge, technology applies it
options.C: Science relies on beliefs, technology on myths
options.D: Science is imaginative, technology is factual

questions.explanation: Science seeks knowledge, while technology applies scientific knowledge for practical purposes.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true about hypotheses?

options.A: They are proven facts
options.B: They are tentative explanations that can be tested

questions.explanation: A hypothesis is a tentative explanation that can be tested through experiments.

questions.question: Which branch of science studies matter and its properties?

options.A: Physics

questions.explanation: Chemistry deals with the study of matter, its properties, and reactions.

questions.question: Which example best illustrates applied science?

options.A: Classifying animals based on characteristics

questions.explanation: Applied science uses knowledge to create practical solutions, like a water purifier.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a step in the scientific method?

options.A: Observation

questions.explanation: Guesswork without evidence is not part of the scientific method.

questions.question: What role does experimentation play in science?

options.A: It tests hypotheses under controlled conditions

questions.explanation: Experiments allow scientists to test hypotheses under controlled, repeatable conditions.

questions.question: Which scientist is known for contributions to electromagnetism?

options.A: Michael Faraday

questions.explanation: Michael Faraday made key contributions to electromagnetism.

questions.question: Which of the following illustrates cause-and-effect reasoning in science?

options.A: Plant growth increases when exposed to sunlight
options.B: The sky is blue because it looks nice
options.C: Dogs bark because they are cute
options.D: Rocks are heavy because they exist

questions.explanation: Cause-and-effect reasoning links sunlight exposure to increased plant growth.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a controlled experiment?

options.A: Testing plant growth with one group getting sunlight and another in shade

questions.explanation: A controlled experiment changes only one variable to study its effect.

questions.question: Why is replication important in scientific experiments?

options.A: To confirm results are reliable and not by chance

questions.explanation: Replication ensures results are reproducible and reliable.

questions.question: Which of the following represents scientific reasoning?

options.A: Predicting weather based on cloud patterns

questions.explanation: Scientific reasoning involves using evidence to make predictions.

questions.question: Which branch of science deals with Earth and its processes?

options.A: Geology

questions.explanation: Geology studies the Earth, its structure, and processes.

questions.question: Which of these statements illustrates a scientific prediction?

options.A: It will rain tomorrow based on clouds

questions.explanation: Scientific predictions are based on observations and evidence.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of data collection?

options.A: Recording daily temperature for a month
options.C: Saying it will be hot because it is summer

questions.explanation: Collecting observations systematically forms scientific data.

questions.question: Which of the following demonstrates the evolving nature of science?

options.A: New evidence changes previous theories
options.D: Once written, a theory is permanent

questions.explanation: Science evolves as new evidence improves or changes our understanding.

questions.question: Which of these shows an application of scientific knowledge?

options.A: Developing vaccines from lab research

questions.explanation: Applied science uses knowledge to solve real-world problems.

questions.question: Why is evidence important in science?

options.A: It supports conclusions and ensures reliability

questions.explanation: Evidence supports scientific conclusions and helps verify results.

questions.question: Which of the following represents a testable question?

options.A: Does sunlight affect plant growth?

questions.explanation: Testable questions can be investigated with experiments and observations.

questions.question: Which example shows correlation but not necessarily causation?

options.A: Ice cream sales increase when temperature rises
options.C: Dropping an object causes it to fall

questions.explanation: Correlation does not always mean one causes the other.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of experimental evidence?

options.A: Observing that seeds grow faster with water
options.B: Believing plants grow better when sung on the full moon
options.C: Saying plants grow better because they look nice

questions.explanation: Experimental evidence is obtained through controlled experiments.

questions.question: Which of these illustrates a scientific investigation?

options.A: Testing the effect of fertilizer on plant growth
options.B: Believing a story about magic plants
options.C: Assuming plants grow better without testing

questions.explanation: Scientific investigations involve systematic testing and observation.
